title: picea likiangensis subsp. bhutanica
intro: Picea likiangensis subsp. bhutanica, also known as Bhutan spruce or Bhutan fir, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the Himalayan foothills. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Picea likiangensis subsp. bhutanica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
